title: Quiz: The Fock Matrix
description: Test your understanding of Fock matrix structure and construction

questions.question: What does the one-electron part (h) of the Fock matrix contain?
questions.explanation: The core Hamiltonian h contains the kinetic energy (-½∇²) and nuclear-electron attraction (-Σₐ Zₐ/rₐ). The two-electron part G contains Coulomb and Exchange contributions.

questions.question: How many unique two-electron integrals are there for N basis functions (using all symmetries)?
questions.explanation: Two-electron integrals have 8-fold permutation symmetry for real orbitals. This reduces the number of unique integrals from N⁴ to approximately N⁴/8.

questions.question: What is the Schwarz inequality used for in Fock matrix construction?

options.text: To screen out negligibly small integrals

questions.explanation: The Schwarz inequality |(μν|λσ)| ≤ √(μν|μν)√(λσ|λσ) provides an upper bound. Integrals below a threshold can be skipped, often eliminating 90%+ of calculations.

questions.question: What scaling does density fitting achieve for two-electron integrals?
questions.explanation: Density fitting (RI) reduces the scaling from O(N⁴) to O(N³) by approximating (μν|λσ) ≈ Σₚₚ (μν|P)(P|Q)⁻¹(Q|λσ) using an auxiliary basis.

questions.question: In UHF, alpha and beta electrons share the same Fock matrix.
questions.explanation: False. In UHF, alpha and beta electrons have separate Fock matrices (Fᵅ and Fᵝ) because exchange only occurs between same-spin electrons.

questions.question: What do diagonal Fock matrix elements (Fμμ) approximately represent?

options.text: The energy of an electron in AO χμ

questions.explanation: Diagonal elements Fμμ approximate the energy of an electron in atomic orbital χμ, including the mean-field electron-electron repulsion. They're most negative for tightly bound core orbitals.

questions.question: Why does the Fock matrix become sparse for large molecules?

options.text: Distant basis functions have negligible overlap and interaction

questions.explanation: Gaussian basis functions decay exponentially with distance, so overlaps and integrals involving well-separated functions are negligible. This creates sparsity in F for extended systems.

questions.question: Which techniques improve Fock matrix construction efficiency? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: a) Schwarz screening eliminates small integrals. b) Density fitting reduces scaling to O(N³). d) Shell batching improves cache efficiency. Larger basis sets (c) increase cost, not efficiency.
